name: Naval Air Facility Atsugi
tagline: A U.S. Navy and Japanese naval air base in the Tokyo metropolitan area.
_note: True OCONUS (Japan). OHA housing, DoDEA Pacific schools, command-sponsorship/SOFA notes, no BAH/RentCast/SchoolDigger/rent-buy. NAF Atsugi is a joint US Navy / Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force air base in greater Tokyo (Kanagawa Prefecture), near Yokosuka. DoDEA: Shirley Lanham Elementary (Atsugi); older students typically attend nearby DoDEA schools (Zama/Yokosuka area). Medical: branch clinic (under USNH Yokosuka). Facts verified from official sources (cnic.navy.mil NAF Atsugi, USNH Yokosuka, DoDEA Pacific, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx); HS arrangement.

data.Branch: U.S. Navy (joint with the Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force)
data.Role: Naval aviation support in the Tokyo area
data.Nearby: Fleet Activities Yokosuka and Camp Zama

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Naval Air Facility Atsugi is a joint U.S. Navy and Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force air base in the Tokyo metropolitan area, supporting naval aviation and the Navy's presence in Japan, near Fleet Activities Yokosuka.
It puts families in the greater Tokyo area, with one of the world's great cities and Japan's superb rail network at hand.

sections.title: A Tokyo-Area Tour: On-Base vs. Living on the Economy

data.subtitle: Atsugi is a Tokyo-metro tour. The main choice is on-base housing versus renting on the Japanese economy with OHA, in a dense urban area where many rely on trains.
intro: Most families come command-sponsored and choose between on-base housing and renting on the Japanese economy with the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA).

On base is the simplest option with schools and services; living on the economy means deeper immersion in Japanese and Tokyo-metro life, balanced against a Japanese lease, the language, and navigating a dense area (many rely on trains over a car).

recommendations.advice: On-base housing keeps you close to schools and services, ideal for a first overseas tour.
Renting on the economy in the surrounding communities offers a deeper Japanese experience, with OHA to offset rent.
The housing office assigns on-base housing and must approve economy rentals, and helps with leases and OHA, so start there.
Japan's rail network makes Tokyo and the whole country accessible; many families rely on transit.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Access follows U.S. and host-nation (SOFA) rules. Visitors without a DoD ID need to be sponsored and registered through the Visitor Control Center.
Requirements can be stricter and change with force-protection levels, so confirm the current details on the official NAF Atsugi site before you go.

sections.title: Housing (On-Base & OHA)
data: NAF Atsugi has on-base family housing, and many families instead rent on the Japanese economy in the surrounding communities, receiving the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) to offset rent and utilities rather than the stateside BAH.
The housing office assigns on-base housing and must approve economy rentals; it also helps with leases, utilities, and OHA. Start there as soon as you have orders.

sections.title: Schools (DoDEA)
data: Children of U.S. personnel attend Department of Defense Education Activity (DoDEA) schools in the Atsugi area (including an on-base elementary school), with older students attending nearby DoDEA schools in the Tokyo-area communities.
Confirm current schools, boundaries, and registration through DoDEA Pacific and the command, and bring records and immunizations when you register.

data.Clinic: Branch Health Clinic Atsugi (under U.S. Naval Hospital Yokosuka)
data.Care: Outpatient primary care on base; inpatient and specialty care via U.S. Naval Hospital Yokosuka and host-nation facilities

intro: An overseas PCS to Japan has extra steps beyond a stateside move. Get these sorted early to settle in smoothly.

steps.title: Confirm command sponsorship
steps.text: Make sure your family is command-sponsored before or upon arrival; it drives your OHA, DoDEA enrollment, and travel entitlements.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your command and finance, update DEERS, and get overseas ID and logistics cards as required.

steps.title: Sort housing and OHA
steps.text: See the housing office first. Choose on-base housing or get approval and help to rent on the economy with OHA; claim Temporary Lodging Allowance (TLA) while you look.

steps.title: Handle SOFA, license, and transport
steps.text: Get your SOFA driver's license and complete SOFA documentation. In the Tokyo metro many families rely on excellent trains; decide whether you need a car.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Register with the DoDEA Atsugi-area schools early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Enroll in the TRICARE Overseas Program so you can be seen at the Atsugi clinic, with U.S. Naval Hospital Yokosuka for specialty and inpatient care.

data.q: Do I get BAH at Atsugi?
data.a: No. Overseas you receive the Overseas Housing Allowance (OHA) for economy rentals, or you live in on-base housing. OHA is based on your actual rent up to a cap, not the stateside BAH tables.
Either in on-base housing or on the Japanese economy in the surrounding Tokyo-area communities using OHA.
Kids attend DoDEA schools in the Atsugi area, with older students at nearby DoDEA schools in the Tokyo-area communities.
Excellent for families: Japan's rail network puts Tokyo and the whole country within reach, and many rely on trains rather than driving.

picks.name: Tokyo
picks.blurb: One of the world's great cities, reachable by train.

picks.name: Yokohama
picks.blurb: A big waterfront city with Chinatown and a famous harbor nearby.

picks.name: Kamakura
picks.blurb: Temples, shrines, and the Great Buddha on the nearby coast.

picks.name: Hakone & Mount Fuji
picks.blurb: Hot springs, lake views, and Mount Fuji to the west.
